2.3.3 使用GDI对象 2.3.4 CDC类绘图成员函数 2.3.5 双缓冲机制 双缓冲机制：所有画图操作将它们画图的结果保存在一块系统内存区域中。在绘图操作结束后，将整块区域复制到显示内存中。 效果：减少画面中的闪烁、撕裂等不良效果，减少画面变化的时间 要求：显存和CPU的消耗时间——缓冲区需要显存，而复制和等待同步需要CPU的计算 实现方法：可以使用操作系统中的页交换？ 2.3.6 设备上下文的调用与释放 2.3.7 MFC绘图的几种方法 2.4 本章小结 第3章 基本图形的扫描转换 3.1 直线的扫描转换 是什么：在屏幕的像素点阵中指定若干像素，得到最接近理想直线的一系列像素点 怎么做：数值微分法DDA（本质是对直线斜率四舍五入）、逐点比较、中点Bresenham算法 3.1.1 算法原理 中点Bresenham算法：每次在主位移方向上走一步，另一个方向上走不走取决于中点偏差判别式 使用直线的隐函数方程F(x,y)=y-kx-b=0 3.1.2 构造中点误差判别式 将两个像素点的中点带入隐函数方程构造偏差判别式d（直接带入就可以得到） d<0，中点在直线下方，则选择y+方向上的像素点；d>0，重点在直线上方，则选择x+方向上的像素点；d=0，中点在直线上，选哪个都可以 3.1.3 递推公式 3.2 圆的扫描转换 是什么：在屏幕的像素点阵中指定若干像素，得到最接近理想圆的一系列像素点 怎么做：中点Bresenham算法 为什么：仅包含加减操作，顺时针绘制1/8圆 3.2.1 算法原理 在I部分，主位移是x方向；在II部分，主位移是y方向 3.2.2 构造中点误差判别式 （略，和直线算法类似） 3.2.3 递推公式 （略，和直线算法类似） 3.3 椭圆的扫描转换 是什么：在屏幕的像素点阵中指定若干像素，得到最接近理想椭圆的一系列像素点 怎么做：中点Bresenham算法 为什么：仅包含加减操作，顺时针绘制1/4椭圆 3.3.1 算法原理 那么与圆的算法类似，只不过利用的是椭圆的隐函数 3.3.2 构造上半部分I的中点误差项 （略，和圆算法类似） 3.3.3 上半部分I的递推公式 （略，和圆算法类似） 3.3.4 构造下半部分II的中点误差项 （略，和圆算法类似） 3.3.5 下半部分II的递推公式 （略，和圆算法类似） 3.4 反走样技术 走样：离散的像素点产生的锯齿效果 反走样技术：使用两行像素来绘制直线，像素的灰度值发生变化 实现方法： 硬件技术：提高显示器分辨率 软件技术：利用人眼视觉，加权参数对灰度级调节 3.5 直线距离加权反走样算法 3.5.1 算法原理 像素到直线的距离作为权重，加权设置像素的灰度值 3.5.2 构造距离误差项 3.5.3 计算机化 d初始为0，主位移方向每走一步d+=k。若d>1，则d–,y++。 3.6 本章小结 第4章 多边形填充 4.1 实面积图形的概念 实面积图形：填充后的多边形，在封闭轮廓内有相同的颜色 4.1.1 多边形的定义 凸多边形、凹多边形、环（中间有洞） 4.1.2 多边形的表示 顶点表示法，点阵（像素点）表示法 多边形扫描转换：从顶点表示法到点阵表示法的变换 4.1.3 多边形的填充 从多边形的顶点出发，求出其覆盖的每个像素点，并进行填充 主要工作：确定穿越多边形内部的扫描线的覆盖区间 4.1.4 填充区域 种子填充算法：从给定的位置开始，按填充颜色点亮相邻像素，直到颜色不同的便捷像素为止 常用方法：4邻接顶点，8邻接顶点 4.2 有效边表填充算法 4.2.1 填充原理 用扫描线来填充多边形 4.2.2 边界像素的处理原则 “下闭上开，左闭右开”【这正是第一节课讨论的问题】 如图，设置一个bool变量（初始为false），每访问一个结点，该变量取反一次。若为true则填充该区间。这样可以避免填充P3P5间像素 4.2.3 有效边与有效边表 有效边：多边形内与当前扫描线相交的边（AE） 为什么引入：仅对AE进行求教计算，避免和多边形所有的边求交 有效边表：把有效边表按照与扫描线交点x坐标递增的顺序存放在链表中（AET） 4.2.4 边表 为什么引入：AET只给出了扫描线和AE交点坐标的计算方式，没有给出新边出现的位置坐标 边表：用于存放扫描线上多边形各条边出现的信息 4.3 边缘填充算法 4.3.1 填充原理 求出多边形的每条边和扫描线的交点，将交点右侧的所有像素颜色取反。按任意顺序处理完多边形的所有边。 4.3.2 填充过程 （略） 算法提速：使用带外接矩形的多边形或者“带栅栏”的多边形（只将交点与栅栏之间的像素取反） 4.4 区域填充算法 4.4.1 填充原理 种子算法 4.4.2 四邻接点与八邻接点 4.4.3 四连通域与八连通域 【这一部分在CV课讲过，比较直观易懂】 4.4.4 种子填充算法 包括四邻接点填充与八邻接点填充。其中四邻接点填充可能无法填充满八连通域。 借助栈实现：种子像素入栈——若栈不为空，则栈顶像素出栈——按填充色绘制出栈像素——从左邻接点开始顺时针取邻接点（四个或八个），若该像素颜色不是边界色并未置为填充色，则入栈——循环到第二步 4.4.5 扫描线种子填充算法 改进方法：沿扫描线对出栈像素左右像素填充，直到遇到边界像素位置。在填充的区间上，检查上下两条相邻的像素是否被填充或者是边界。 4.5 本章小结 第5章 二维变换与裁剪 5.1 图形几何变换基础 5.1.1 规范化齐次坐标 齐次坐标：用n+1维矢量表示n维矢量 好处：图形的几何变换可以表示为图形顶点集合的规范化齐次坐标矩阵与某一变换矩阵相乘的形式 5.1.2 矩阵相乘 5.1.3 二维几何变换矩阵 左上角2x2为比例、旋转、反射、错切变换；左下角2x1位平移变换；右上角1x2位投影变换；右下角的s是整体比例变换 【也有一些教材是将右上角作为平移变换，左下角作为投影变换】 5.1.4 二维几何变换形式 5.2 二维图形基本几何变换矩阵 5.2.1 平移变换矩阵 根据坐标表示的不同，也可能是T的转置矩阵 5.2.2 比例变换矩阵 5.2.3 旋转变换矩阵 根据坐标表示的不同，也可能是T的转置矩阵 5.2.4 反射变换矩阵 5.2.5 错切变换矩阵 5.3 二维复合变换 5.3.1 复合变换原理 多个基本几何变换的组合，本质上是多个变换矩阵的点乘 5.3.2 相对于任意参考点的二维几何变换 5.3.3 相对于任意方向的二维几何变换 5.4 二维图形裁剪 5.4.1 图形学中常用的坐标系 用户坐标系UC：用户定义的原始图形所采用的坐标系，比如二维或三维 观察坐标系VC：依据观察窗口方向和形状在用户坐标系中定义的坐标系 设备坐标系DC：也称屏幕坐标系，都是二维坐标系。通常原点是左上角，x轴水平向右，y轴水平向下 规格化设备坐标系NDC：DC的归一化 5.4.2 窗口与视区 窗口：观察坐标系中定义的确定显示内容的区域 视区：在设备坐标系汇总定义的输出图形的区域 5.4.3 窗视变换矩阵 5.5 Cohen-Sutherland直线段裁剪算法 为什么：需要在观察坐标系下根据窗口的大小对用户坐标系中的二维图形进行裁剪，只将位于窗口内的图形变换到视区输出 5.5.1 编码原理 5.5.2 裁剪步骤 直线的两个端点的区域编码都为0，即二者按位或结果为0，则直线两端点都在窗口内 直线的两个端点的区域编码都不为0，即二者按位与结果不为0，则直线两端点都在窗口外 否则，直线与窗口相交，需要计算交点 5.5.3 交点计算公式 非常简单，只需要将窗口边界的直线方程带入目标直线方程即可 5.6 中点分割直线段裁剪算法 为什么：不用求解交点就可以直接裁剪 5.6.1 中点分割算法原理 简单地把直线段等分为两段直线，用“迭代二分”的思想去完成裁剪 5.6.2 中点计算公式 就是解析几何中的中点公式 5.7 Liang-Barsky直线段裁剪算法 5.7.1 Liang-Barsky裁剪算法原理 利用直线的参数方程，把直线和窗口的相互位置关系划分为两种情况：平行于窗口边界的直线，不平行于窗口边界的直线 5.7.2 算法分析 主要考虑直线方程参数t的变化情况 5.7.3 算法的几何意义 5.8 本章小结 第6章 三维变换与投影 6.1 三维图形几何变换 6.1.1 三维几何变换矩阵 类比二维集合变换矩阵即可 6.1.2 三维几何变换形式 6.2 三维基本几何变换矩阵 6.2.1 平移变换 根据坐标形式不同，有时也表示为T的转置 6.2.2 比例变换 6.2.3 旋转变换 根据绕不同的轴旋转，有不同的形式。根据方程都可以推出。 6.2.4 反射变换 根据对不同轴的反射，有不同的形式。根据方程都可以推出。 6.2.5 错切变换 6.3 三维复合变换 6.3.1 相对于任意参考点的三维几何变换 6.3.2 相对于任意方向的三维几何变换 6.4 投影变换 6.4.1 三视图 针对与某个平面，将其对应的参数变为0 6.4.2 斜等侧图 斜等侧图：将三维物体向投影面做平行投影，但投影方向不垂直于投影面 6.5 透视变换 【这一部分在CV课上有详细讲过，所以简略梳理】 6.5.1 透视变换坐标系 世界坐标系——右手系 观察坐标系——左手系 屏幕坐标系——左手系 6.5.2 世界坐标系到观察坐标系的变换 6.5.3 观察坐标系到屏幕坐标系的变换 6.5.4 透视投影分类 灭点：在透视投影中，平行的两条线也会在远处的某一点相交 一点透视，二点透视，三点透视 6.6 本章小结 第7章 自由曲线与曲面 【这一部分在动画课上有详细介绍，此处简单记录】 7.1 基本概念 7.1.1 曲线与曲面的表示形式 7.1.2 拟合与逼近 拟合：用一组插值点来指定曲线曲面的形状 逼近：用一组控制点来指定曲线曲面的形状 7.1.3 连续性条件 提出的原因：保证曲线段在连接点处平滑过渡 参数连续性：n阶导数相同 几何连续性：只要求参数导数成比例 7.2 三次参数样条曲线 7.2.1 三次参数样条曲线的定义 已知n个型值点，P(t)使得所有型值点在函数P(t)上，整个区间上二阶连续可导且每个子区间里的分段函数都是t的三次多项式 7.2.2 系数求解 7.2.3 边界条件 7.3 Hermite样条曲线 Cardinal样条曲线：规定了两个型值点之间的插值多项式的边界条件 7.4 Bezier曲线 7.4.1 Bezier曲线的定义 以三次Bezier曲线为例 7.4.2 Bezier曲线的性质 端点性质，一阶导数性质（曲线的起始点的切线方向位于控制多变性的起始边和终止边的切线方向上），凸包性质（曲线位于控制多变行构成的凸包之内） 7.4.3 Bezier曲线的可分割性 德卡斯特利奥算法 7.5 Bezier曲面 7.5.1 Bezier曲面的定义 其中Pij是控制点坐标，Bim(u)和Bjn(v)是Bernstein基函数 【在动画课上作业里实现了FFD，所以对这里比较熟悉】 7.5.2 双三次Bezier曲面的定义 7.6 B样条曲线 提出：使用B样条基函数代替Bernstein基函数 优点：曲线更光滑，更贴近控制多边形，曲线的次数可根据需要指定，不用根据控制点的个数来确定；增加了局部修改功能。 7.6.1 B样条曲线的定义 7.6.2 二次B样条曲线 7.6.3 三次B样条曲线 矩阵形式： 常用三次B样条曲线来控制多边形 7.6.4 B样条曲线的性质 连续性；局部可修改性 7.6.5 构造特殊的三次B样条曲线的技巧 两顶点重合法；三顶点重合法；三顶点共线法；四顶点共线法 基本思想：特征三角形退化成点或者一条线 7.7 B样条曲面 7.7.1 B样条曲面的定义 7.7.2 双三次B样条曲面的定义 由三次B样条曲线交织而成。一般情况下不通过控制网格的任何一个顶点。 7.7.3 双三次B样条曲面的连续性 优点：自然地解决了曲面片之间的连接问题 7.8 本章小结 第8章 分形几何 8.1 分形与分维 8.1.1 分形的诞生 8.1.2 分形的基本特征 自相似性；无标度性（不能用长度、质量、面积等标度来描述） 8.1.3 分形的定义 具有任何尺度下的比例细节的，不规则的，具有自相似性和递归特质的图形集 8.1.4 分形维数的定义 D=lnN/lnS，N为局部形体个数，S为相似比 8.2 递归模型 8.2.1 Cantor集 三等分，取两端，递归。数目无穷多，长度趋于0。分形维数D=ln2/ln3=0.6309 8.2.2 Koch曲线 三等分，取两端，中间生成60°夹角的两个等长线段。处处连续，处处不可导。D=ln4/ln3=1.26 8.2.3 Peano-Hilbert曲线 一个可以充满整个平面的一维曲线。D=ln4/ln2=2。 8.2.4 Sierpinski垫片、地毯与海绵 垫片：总周长趋于无穷，总面积趋于0。D=ln3/ln2=1.5849 地毯：D=ln8/ln3=1.8927 海绵：体积有限，表面积无限。D=ln20/ln3=2.7288 8.2.5 C字曲线 8.2.6 Cayley树 8.3 L系统模型 8.3.1 L系统文法构图法 即乌龟几何：给定初始点，朝向角，前进距离。 8.3.2 Koch曲线 8.3.3 分形草 【根据乌龟几何算法和分形的原理，可以提出一种对于植物的模拟方式】 8.3.4 Peano-Hilbert曲线 8.3.5 分形灌木丛 8.4 IFS迭代函数系统模型 8.4.1 仿射变换 8.4.2 IFS 压缩仿射变换 8.4.3 Koch曲线 8.4.4 Sierpinski垫片 8.4.5 Barnsley蕨 8.4.6 拼贴与IFS码的确定 8.5 本章小结 第9章 建模与消隐 9.1 三维物体的数据结构 9.1.1 物体的几何信息与拓扑信息 几何信息：描述几何元素空间位置的信息 拓扑信息：描述几何元素之间相互连接关系的信息 9.1.2 三表结构 顶点表，边表，面表 9.1.3 立体表示模型 线框模型，表面模型，实体模型 9.2 消隐算法分类 隐线算法，隐面算法 还有一种分类方式：物空间消隐算法、像空间消隐算法、物像空间消隐算法 9.3 隐线算法 9.3.1 凸多面体消隐算法 关键：给出测试其表面边界线可见性的判别式 判断依据：外法矢量N和视矢量S的夹角θ 当0°<θ<90°，立方体表面可见；当θ=90°，立方体表面退化为一条直线；当90°<θ<180°，立方体表面不可见。 9.3.2 曲面体消隐算法 以球面为例 球面坐标表示为(Rsinφcosθ,Rsinφsinθ,Rcosφ) 还是以外法矢量N和视矢量S的数量积作为检测条件 外法矢量：根据边矢量P0P1和P0P2的分量计算 视矢量：根据球面坐标和任意四边形平面片上的点计算 9.4 隐面算法 9.4.1 Z-Buffer算法（深度缓冲器算法） 像素级的以近物取代远物的消隐算法。 在物空间不对例题进行可见性检测，每个面的像素点均以指定颜色全部绘制出来，在像空间中根据深度值确定最终绘制体各个表面的像素点颜色。 两个缓冲器：深度缓冲器——记录立体上每一个像素的深度值，初始化为立体的最小深度值；帧缓冲器——记录立体上每个像素点的颜色值，初始化为屏幕的背景色。 对立体的各个表面全部进行采样，将采样点变换到屏幕坐标系空间，记录深度z的坐标值。根据CIA样点在屏幕上的投影位置，将其深度与已存储在帧缓存器中相应像素点处的深度值进行比较。新采样点深度值>原可见点深度值，则更新；否则不更改。 如何计算采样点深度值？——有效边表算法访问面上的每个像素点 9.4.2 画家算法（深度排序算法） 动态画家算法：每一次改变立体的转角后，动态计算每个面上所有各像素的深度值，将最小深度值作为该面的深度值，并且排序从小到大绘制各个面。 补充：A缓存算法 每个单元包含两个域：深度域和强度域。可以处理透明表面，并对对象的边界进行反走样处理 补充：BSP树算法 补充：Warnock算法 采用分治的思想，利用堆栈的数据结构。把物体投影到全屏窗口上，递归分割窗口，直到窗口内的目标足够简单到可以显示（包含/相交/包围/分离） 9.5 本章小结 第10章 真实感图形 10.1 颜色模型 10.1.1 基本概念 颜色的取决因素：物体本身、光源、环境、观察者的视觉系统 10.1.2 RGB颜色模型 RGBA模型：alpha因子决定了各个颜色为融合操作的贡献比例 10.1.3 Gouraud颜色渐变 Gouraud模型：对多边形顶点的颜色进行插值以生成中间各点的颜色 处理基础：有效边表算法 10.2 材质模型和光照模型 10.2.1 物体的材质 材质：物体表面对光的反射特性 10.2.2 环境光模型 环境光的效果：物体在不受光源直射的情况下也有一定的亮度 环境光：环境中其他物体散射到该物体表面后再反射出来的光 10.2.3 漫反射光模型 漫反射光：点光源照射下，光被物体表面吸收后，重新反射出来的光（一点照射，多方向反射，均匀散布，与观察者位置无关） Lambert定理：物体漫反射的光强同入射光与物体表面法线之间的夹角余弦成正比 10.2.4 镜面反射光模型 镜面反射光：高光区域呈现的效果，只朝一定方向反射的光 Phong模型：对多边形顶点的法矢量进行插值以生成中间各点的法矢量 其中H是L和V的平分矢量 10.2.5 光强衰减 10.3 纹理映射 10.3.1 纹理定义 UV纹理空间 10.3.2 纹理映射 将UV纹理坐标映射到三维景物坐标 补充：纹理的插值方式 最近邻插值，双线性插值，双三次插值（利用三次方程进行两次插值，效果好，速度慢） 补充：Mipmap技术 为了加快渲染速度和减少图像锯齿，贴图被处理成由一系列被预先计算和优化过的图片组成的文件，即mipmap。mipmap的压缩遵循小波采样技术。 为什么能提速？——mipmap贴图需要读取的像素小。不同层级的贴图尺寸不一，且经过抗锯齿处理，减少了实时渲染开销，同时放缩也会更高效。 10.4 OpenGL简介 （略） 10.5 本章小结 补充：全局光照相关知识 （参考《计算机图形学（第四版）》） 光线跟踪算法 参考：https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/144403005 Whitted光照明模型 提出原因：Blinn-Phong模型属于局部模型，无法处理群居效果 算法步骤 从视点出发通过该像素中心向场景发出一条光线R，求出R与场景中所有物体的全部交点 获取离视点最近的交点P。根据局部光照模型计算P出的颜色值Ic（光线投射） 在P处沿着R镜面反射方向和投射方向各衍生一条光线 对衍生光线递归执行前面的步骤。计算这两个方向上周围环境对点P光亮度的贡献Is和It 依据Whitted光照明模型计算P出亮度 光线跟踪递归终止条件 光线与环境中任何物体均不相交，或交于纯漫射面 被跟踪光线返回的光亮度值对像素颜色的贡献很小 已递归到给定深度 求交检测 光线与隐式曲面求交：将光线方程带入求解t即可 光线与显式曲面求交——转换成求直线与三角形面的交点 光线跟踪加速算法 层次包围体：使用层次结构细分包围盒，减少光线与物体的求交检测时间复杂度。用树状结构管理可以减少到O(logn) 均匀格点：将场景分成均匀的网格，对每一个格点，将所有与其相交的面片记录下来，存储为一个列表，当光线与小网格中面片相交时，则求对应的交点，不经过的就不用求 四叉树/八叉树：以八叉树为例，二叉树的三维推广，递归地分成八个小立方体进行遍历。 BSP树 分布式光线跟踪：在一点出发射多条光线 辐射度光照模型 用能量角度描述漫反射 辐射能术语 光子的能量，单色光辐射的总能量，光谱辐射能（某一频率的光的辐射能量），光通量（光能，即单位时间内传递的辐射能总量），辐射度（单位面积离开表面的光通量），强度（特定方向，单位立体角 ，单位投影面积的光通量） 基本辐射度模型 补充：着色器相关知识 GLSL语言 glsl语言是一种类C语言，为图形计算量身定制的，它包含一些针对向量和矩阵操作的有用特性。 glsl语言包括C的大多数数据类型，还支持向量和矩阵两种容器。每个着色器都可以独立输入输出，进行高效的数据交流和传递。glsl定义了in和out来实现输入输出。只要一个输出变量与下一个着色器阶段的输入匹配，就会传递下去。 顶点着色器：从顶点数据中直接接受输入。使用location这一元数据指定输入变量。一般我们用额外的layout标识来将其链接到顶点数据。 片元着色器需要一个vec4颜色输出变量，因为片元着色器需要生成一个最终输出的颜色。综上，我们需要在发送方着色器中声明一个输出，在接收方着色器中声明一个类似的输入。当二者类型、名字一致时，OpenGL就会在链接程序对象时将其链接在一起。 Uniform是一种从CPU应用向GPU着色器发送数据的方式。uniform是全局的，全局意味着uniform变量必须在每个着色器程序对象中都是独一无二的，而且它可以被着色器程序的任意着色器在任意阶段访问，然后，无论你把uniform值设置成什么，uniform会一直保存它们的数据，直到它们被重置或更新。 图形渲染管线 图形渲染管线指的是对一些原始数据经过一系列的处理变换并最终把这些数据输出到屏幕上的整个过程。 顶点数据是一些顶点的集合，顶点一般是3维的点坐标组成。 **基本图元（Primitives）**包括点，线段，三角形等，是构成实体模型的基本单位，需要在传入顶点数据的同时通知OpenGL这些顶点数据要组成的基本图元类型。 **顶点着色器（Vertex Shader）**包含对一些顶点属性（数据）的基本处理。 **基本图元装配（Primitive Assembly）**把所有输入的顶点数据作为输入，输出制定的基本图元。 **几何着色器（Geometry Shader）**把基本图元形式的顶点的集合作为输入，可以通过产生新顶点构造出新的（或是其他的）基本图元来生成其他形状。 **细分着色器（Tessellation Shaders）**可以把基本图元细分为更多的基本图形，创建出更加平滑的视觉效果。 **光栅化（Rasterization）**即像素化，把细分着色器输出的基本图形映射为屏幕上网格的像素点，生成供片段着色器处理的片段（Fragment），光栅化包含一个剪裁操作，会舍弃超出定义的视窗之外的像素。 **片段着色器（Fragment Shader）**的主要作用是计算出每一个像素点最终的颜色，通常片段着色器会包含3D场景的一些额外的数据，如光线，阴影等。 测试与混合是对每个像素点进行深度测试，Alpha测试等测试并进行颜色混合的操作，这些测试与混合操作决定了屏幕视窗上每个像素点最终的颜色以及透明度。

title: Spoofer On Attack: An Exploration of Email Sender Authentication
text: Spoofer On Attack: The Exploration of Email Sender Authentication 1. Introduction and Relative Work 1.1 Background Component-based software design is a primary engineering approach for building modern software systems. When various components work together, because of different interpretations of messages or working protocols, the inconsistence between components may lead to insecurity. As the well-known mail transfer protocol, SMTP lacked mechanisms to protect the process of email sending and receiving. In an email, there are many identities, such as MAIL FROM, HELO, Sender and so on. But SMTP does not enforce consistencies among these which may lead to insecurity. To prevent email spoofing and other security issue, three protocols —— SPF, DKIM, and DMARC are put into use. But inconsistencies between different components could lead to security vulnerabilities. Because the verification of each step is separate. The attackers can exploit the inconsistencies between different internal components in an e-mail system. To use the mail system freely, we aim to achieve some security requirement, that is the end-user Bob who uses email client C to receive an email from receiving server R can determine that the message is indeed from user Alice of sending server S. Under this expectation, we are supposed to prove or ensure that (1) The From header of the email that S sends matches the authenticated username (other users of S cannot spoof Alice’s address); (2) SPF/DKIM/DMARC components in R can obtain S’s DNS correct policy; (3) SPF/DKIM and DMARC components in R consistently authenticate the same identifier; (4) the identifier that R authenticates is consistent with the identifier that C shows to Bob. But in reality, there exist packs of challenges. The first is working between different components. Although each specific system is fully functional, but when they work at the same time, something terrible may happen. The second is tensions with the robust principle, when facing ambiguous circumstance or potentially dangerous client, the actions different components take can lead to numerous email spoofing attacks. The last one is the danger of feature composition which is similar to the first one. Because of the hidden problem, we are still under threat. Our work is to focus on such problem and look into several related paper and do a study on this topic about e-mail security. 1.1.1 SMTP SMTP is the standard protocol for email services on a TCP/IP network.it provides the ability to send and receive email messages. However, SMTP lacks authentication. In practice, attackers usually exploit SMTP by running their own email servers or clients. SMTP’s design includes multiple “identities” when handling messages.it also introduces multiple other sender identities, thus leading to the question: which identity to authenticate. figure 1.1: An example of an SMTP message 1.1.2 SPF/DKIM/DMARC To combat email forgery, various email authentication mechanisms have been developed, including SPF, DKIM, and DMARC. Sender Policy Framework (SPF) allows a domain owner to publish DNS records to specify which servers are allowed to send emails for the domain. A major problem of SPF is incompatibility with mail forwarders. figure 1.2: An example of SPF work process Domain Keys Identified Mail (DKIM) uses cryptography to authenticate senders and protect email integrity. The general idea behind DKIM is to let senders sign parts of messages so that receivers can validate them. figure 1.3: An example of DKIM work process However,neither SPF nor DKIM provides a complete solution for preventing email spoofing. Neither of them authenticates the From header displayed to the end-user, so that the FROM address of emails can still be forged. Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting & Conformance (DMARC) is designed to fix the final trust problem by building on SPF and DKIM. The target is to ensure that the address in the FROM header cannot be forged and prevents email forgery. 1.2 Threat Model We assume three types of attackers who would be able to exploit the vulnerability of mail server: Forgery attacker. They may spoof the email’s sender in the From header and craft a fake message to attack the victim’s mailbox. Replay attacker. They may use the valid SPF record and DKIM signatures to bypass the security system and exploit modifications to email header and send the fake email. Malicious mailbox owner. They may own a legitimate email address to pass all the authentication. However they also have the ability to cheat the security system and exploit the failure of email providers to spoof the email header. Based on the previous types of attackers, various of attacking methods could be raised to cheat the SPF/DKIM/DMARC security system. 2. Discussion on Paper Work We choose the paper Composition Kills: A Case Study of Email Sender Authentication as the main reference. In this section, we’ll do the basic study on this awarded paper. 2.1 Researching method To investigate how real-world systems handle these challenges, the research team conducted a security analysis of popular email providers and MUAs. In total, the team tested 10 email providers and 19 MUAs, including 9 local email clients and 10 web interfaces. The team defined an email authentication mechanism as broken when the following both hold: 1) the email server erroneously verifies the test email’s sender as not spoofed, for example, DMARC authentication produces a “pass” result; 2) the MUA erroneously indicates that the sender address is from a (legitimate) target domain rather than the attacker’s sending domain. Next, we will discover the way to attack the email system introduced in the paper, and show the test results of popular email server and MUAs facing many kinds of attacking techniques. Each technique is marked by letters such as A1 or B1. 2.2 Attacking Methods Raised by Paper 2.2.1 Intra-server attacks Intra-server attacks exploit inconsistencies between different internal components of a single implementation. The team discovered three techniques to exploit their inconsistencies: (1) HELO/MAIL FROM confusion (A1, A2); (2) ambiguous domains (A3); and (3) authentication results injection (A4, A5). HELO/MAIL FROM confusion (A1, A2). SMTP employs two different identifiers—HELO and MAIL FROM—to represent the email sender who transmits a message. And when DMARK and SPF work, SPF is supposed to check the both, but actually HELO is recommended instead of enforced, while DMARK use MAIL FROM identities to perform alignment test. This design introduces the possibility that different components might authenticate different identifiers. Non-existent subdomains (A1). The first technique crafts a MAIL FROM domain as a non-existent subdomain of a legitimate domain. That means the domain in MAIL FROM is not exist at all, SPF can only test the HELO, which is under the control of attackers, and forwards a “pass” result. However, still use the MAIL FROM domain to perform the alignment test with the FROM header. “Empty” MAIL FROM addresses (A2). Some SPF implementations treat “(any@legitimate.com” as an empty MAIL FROM address, and leads to the same result as A1. Ambiguous domains (A3), NUL ambiguity (A3). DKIM and DNS components differ in interpreting NULs in domain name. Authentication results injection. To explain this attack technique, firstly we will simply introduce authentication result header syntax which is passed by SPF and DKIM to DMARK to show the verification result. In the authentication result header, “smtp.mailfrom” represents the domain verified by the SPF component, and “header.d” represents the domain verified by the DKIM component. A vulnerability arises because attackers can modify the domain name embedded in the “smtp.mailfrom” and “header.d”. Particularly, attackers can introduce malformed domains including meta-characters, which will be treated as data by SPF and DKIM, and be parsed as control information by DMARC. The authentication results injection can be divided into DKIM authentication results injection (A4) and SPF authentication results injection (A5) which is similar. The paper issues the test results of their work. It shows that many email providers are proofed to have such vulnerability to Intra-server attacks up to the publication date. figure 2.1: The test on main mailboxes provided by the paper author 2.2.2 UI-mismatch Generally, UI-mismatch attacks exploit the inconsistencies between how an email server validates a message versus how the MUA ultimately indicates its validity. We divide the UI-mismatch attacks into two categories: ambiguous FROM headers and ambiguous email addresses. Ambiguous FROM headers exploits the inconsistencies on the display and authenticate rules on FROM header of a certain email. Multiple From headers (A6). When an email has multiple FROM headers, the email massage should be rejected by receiving services. For example, some of the MUAs show last FROM header while others use the first one. Space-surrounded From headers (A7). The attackers violate the email header syntax by inserting whitespace around the FROM headers which will be handled by email servers in different way. First, use of whitespace can bypass the email server’s validation. Second, inconsistent interpretation of whitespace can lead to ambiguities. From alternative headers (A8). If an attacker crafts an email with no From header or an unrecognized From header, some implementations will use alternative headers to identify the message sender. Next we will discuss ambiguous email addresses. A valid From header consists of four elements, with a single mailbox address. The four elements are Display name, Real address, Route portion and comment. Attackers can exploit the complexity of FROM header syntax. Attacks leveraging complex From headers. The team find that implementations vary in parsing and interpreting From headers. Here we show five attacks that exploit these inconsistencies. Multiple email addresses (A9). The team observe 5 distinct behaviors in processing From headers listing multiple addresses. Gmail.com (Server) and Mail.ru (Server) reject the messages; Tutanota.com (Web) displays the last address; Zoho.com (Server) and iCloud.com (Web) don’t verify or display any address; 2 mail servers and 4 MUAs verify or display all of the addresses; all the others take the first address. Email address encoding (A10). In the experiments, Yahoo.com (Server), Outlook.com (Server), iCloud.com (Server), Fastmail (Server), Zoho.com (Server) and Tutanota.com (Server) don’t recognize the encoded address, and use attack.com for DMARC testing; but Gmail.com (Web), Outlook.com (Web), Yahoo.com (Web), Naver.com (Web), Mail (MacOS), Mail (Windows) and Mail (iOS) support this encoding feature, and only display the first address. Route portion (A11). Fastmail.com (Server) does not recognize the route portion, and treats attack.com as a real address to use for DMARC verification; while 10 MUAs, including Fastmail.com (Web), ignore the route portion, and only show admin@legitimate.com. Quoted-pairs (A12). Gmail.com (Server) and iCloud.com (Web) recognize the second address; but Mail (Windows), iCloud (Server) and 12 other implementations only use the first one. Parsing inconsistencies (A13). Thunderbird (Linux), Mail.ru (Web), Gmail.com (Server) and Mail.ru (Server) mistakenly validate or display admin@legitimate.com as the real sender but Outlook.com (Server), iCloud.com (Server), Protonmail.com (Server) and 9 other implementations recognize it as the wrong one. SPF, DKIM, and DMARC rely on domain queries for sender authentication. When failing to obtain the domain record, the mail service providers may decide that the domain doesn’t deploy the corresponding security mechanisms, and allow the message into the user’s inbox. The attackers can use Invisible characters (B1), Encoding (B2), From alternative headers (B3) to exploit the vulnerability. The test results in the paper implies a more serious and general problem on most mailboxes. figure 2.2: The test on main mailboxes focus on the UI-mismatch attacks provided by the paper author 2.2.3 Ambiguous-replay Attacks This is similar to the replay attack, the attackers spoof emails with seemingly valid DKIM signatures from legitimate domains. Two DKIM mechanisms make signature spoofing possible. First, DKIM doesn’t prevent replay attacks. Second, DKIM allows attackers to append additional email headers— or even body contents, in some cases—to the original message. Next, we will discuss DKIM signature replay attacks. DKIM signatures protect both email headers and bodies. The latter is always signed. Signing headers, however, is optional, and specified by the “h=” tag of the DKIM-Signature header. Header spoofing (A14 and A15). Among the various identities, only the From header must be signed. A replay attacker can modify these unprotected fields in signed messages without invalidating DKIM signatures. While including all necessary headers in the signature can prevent attackers from tampering them, an attacker can craft ambiguous emails by adding a new header (e.g., Subject) to the signed mail, if two parties in the email process chain parse and interpret the extra header differently. Body spoofing (A16). The attack technique can modify the email body without destroying the DKIM signature. The DKIM-Signature header has the option “l=”tag which indicates the length of the email body included in the signature. By modify the tag and email body, attackers can change the content of email without being detected by email servers. The problem of spoofing via an email account (A17) arises when an email provider does not perform sufficient checks on the From header, enabling an attacker to send a signed message with another user’s address (e.g., administrator). As the message has the email provider’s DKIM signature attached, it will pass the receiver’s DKIM and DMARC validation. The spoofing attack (A18). First the attackers craft normal email to the email servers to get the DKIM signature and reuse the signature by adding an extra From header with another user’s address to the DKIM-signed message and resends it to a victim. 2.3 More Discussion The team mainly discuss about the vulnerability arise from the inconsistence between different parts of authentication. The exploration pay attention to the mechanism with defective and unreasonable design. So, we can also pay close attention to the users’ manner and using habit. Because with a large number of users, millions of mail transfer may present specific behaviors which may lead to the insecurity. Based on the big data analysis, we may find other vulnerabilities which could be exploited by the attackers. 3. Spoofer Demo After looking into the paper and understand the learning method, we constructed an email spoofer demo to illustrated our research results. The demo has two core part. One is the SMTP-based email sender and the other is the spoofing parameters. By setting the spoofing parameters, it is possible to send a sender-spoofed email. The demo is written in Python. Several open-sourced library are used, such as dns, pydkim and socket. One matter to be issued: All the spoofing cases are tested based on personal domain and personal email address. Besides, the email systems are all strengthened, so it cannot reach the target that achieve a real spoofing. The test is a way to proof that such technique is able to crack SPF/DKIM/DMARC authentication. 3.1 SMTP Framework The implementation of SMTP is based on TCP and socket. The logic could be simplified as the figure below. figure 3.1: SMTP workflow The first step is common TCP three-way handshake. After the connection was established between sender and the mail server, the sender will send a EHLO request packet to ask for the server response. EHLO is an extended version of HELO, which supports user authentication. EHLO request includes the login information. A typical EHLO section can be listed as follow: 1234567891011121314<<< ehlo 126.com // inform the server to do the identity authentication>>> 250-mail 250-PIPELINING 250-AUTH LOGIN PLAIN 250-AUTH=LOGIN PLAIN 250-coremail 1Uxr2xKj7kG0xkI17xGrU7I0s8FY2U3Uj8Cz28x1UUUUU7Ic2I0Y2UrTiPGSUCa0xDrUUUUj 250-STARTTLS 250 8BITMIME <<< AUTH LOGIN aml*****29t // start identifying, sending the user mail address with base64 encoding>>> 334 UGFzc3dvcmQ6 // asking for password<<< ZW**********Mz // the password with base64 encoding>>> 235 Authentication successful After the identity authentication, SMTP server will check the MAIL FROM header and the RCPT TO header. This will be the last security defense of SMTP. 3.2 Key Techniques 3.2.1 Domain Setting In order to perform well both on authenticated email address and the invalid domain (which refers to the fake email address), we used a personal domain name ruin.net.cn to deploy several configuration. Considering the security of email system, most of the servers shut down the 25 port which is the default forwarding port of SMTP. Thus, this domain cannot be set as an email sender. However, by changing the parameter of the email message and configuring the DNS records, a message with invalid domain name could be sent to the victim email. 3.2.2 SPF & DKIM Signature Configuration By setting SPF record and DKIM signature to a personal domain, the certain domain would be able to pass the SPF and DKIM checking. Generally, simple DNS resolver could achieve this goal. An SPF record can be noted as follow: 1@ TXT v=spf1 a mx ip4:222.205.46.0/24 -all A DKIM signature record can be noted as follow: 1selector._domainkey TXT v=DKIM1; k=rsa; p=MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQCMK4X+VibGhNWBGmxwvNgTKqyyLHuOWlomLo0vG9nhy97w8BLUz6fD6yLYdVhmrWW8hy8j0rcPxr40+L6P9yZ5yDpYwTwcarEky8oj4Jb2XlqWE9OcYgIOjpoN5d2xGqJau+S0bmeuneuMRPh4cx/Uo1qEovsxnMAAiKPgmze6nwIDAQAB The p domain in DKIM record refers to the public key. The private key is attached to the message while sending to the SMTP receiver. 3.3 Typical Spoofing Methods Since most of the mail service providers have already strengthened their guarding system, it’s hard to recover the results presented by the author. The following tests are performed on a personal Outlook mail address, and all tests only proof that such a spoofing method may be effective to pass the DMARC checking. 3.3.1 A1 Attack: Fake subdomain invasion The case crafts a fake MAIL FROM header with non-existent subdomain to cheat the receiver. Since some SPF implementations only check the HELO header, the MAIL FROM header is able to be a spoofing point. As long as the MAIL FROM address is not empty, OpenDMARC authentication will return a positive result because of the pass of SPF checking. figure 3.2: A1 Attack mail figure 3.3: A1 Attack mail check In our test, the fake subdomain can still pass the verify of DKIM test while the SPF check shows none because of the MAIL FROM is set to a fake subdomain name fakedoain.ruin.net.cn. However the DKIM check still go through the genuine domain name ruin.net.cn. The DMARC check result is bestguesspass for there’s no DMARC result setting at outlook.com. While no error reported from both SPF and DKIM, it gave a reasonable infer that the mail could pass the DMARC testing. 3.3.2 A5 Attack: The variation of authentication results injection This case is a variation of the A5 Attack in the paper. This case exploit the vulnerability of the attacker’s accessibility and the character’s non-consistent. In particular, an attacker can introduce malformed domains that include meta-characters. Different secure protocol and mail servers may treat those characters differently. In this spoofing case, the address in MAIL FROM is routed to another address. For example, a MAIL FROM header <@legitimate.com,@any.com:'any@attack.com> will lead to an inconsistence display in some mail server. Take outlook.com as an example, the receiver will be shown as any@legitimate.com, while the spoofed email will pass the security by the domain name attack.com. figure 3.4: A5 Attack mail figure 3.5: A5 Attack mail check The result shows that such a spoofed email with a fake MAIL FROM header will pass both SPF and DKIM. The verify system check only the legitimate domain, while the showing result is the attack site. 3.3.3 A10 Attack: Email address encoding This case exploits the difference of coding rules between different part. The basic coding rule of a SMTP email is US-ASCII characters. To support non-ASCII characters, RFC 2047 defined two encoding approaches: Base64 encoding and quoted-printable encoding with following syntax: 1=?charset?encoding?encoded-text?= Thus a hidden From header can be added to the mail, for example: 1=?utf-8?B? +base64.b64encode(b"admin@126.com")+ ?= Inside which is a base64 encoding of an email address, which will be displayed on MUA. figure 3.6: A10 Attack mail figure 3.7: A10 Attack mail check With this attacking method, we can even spoof as another domain. The SPF will go pass the sender address, which is outside the base64 encoding. While the displayed one is the fake spoofed sender address which encoded by base64. 3.3.4 A13 Attack: Parsing inconsistencies This is one of the methods that leverages complex From headers. In paper, the author came up with various of spoofing methods including using multiple email address, encoding the email address and so on. We implemented the spoofing method which exploits the parsing inconsistencies. This is based on the inconsistencies in recognizing the precedence of different delimiters. For some email server, DMARC component will take ‘<’ character for higher priority and go through the authentication process while MUA will have a different preference and display the other one. figure 3.8: A13 Attack mail figure 3.9: A13 Attack mail check The testing result shows that the SPF and DMARC all go through the spoofed email address. 3.3.5 A17 Attack: Spoofing via an email service account Among all of the test cases, the most reliable attack methods is to spoof via an authenticated account. In this way, the only goal is to forge the sender address. The most effective way is to forge into another email address of the same email server of the attacker’s account. figure 3.10: A17 Attack mail figure 3.11: A17 Attack mail check Our test case uses a personal email account in 126.com and the goal is the result is to cheat the MUA and only display the spoofed email address admin@126.com. The email provider does not perform sufficient checks on the From header, enabling an attacker to send a signed message with another user’s address. As the message has the email provider’s DKIM signature attached, it will pass the receiver’s DKIM and DMARC validation. 4. Further Study 4.1 Basic Conclusion The protocol allows the sender to send a mail with multi-identifier. Although we can manager it with strict definition, implementation of SMTP always cant’s fully understand the complicated definition. Complicated syntax rules based on text leads to the inconsistency between different components and the ambiguity of parsing process. Allowing various types of white space and notes makes the possible positions variable. But different implementations would not obey the rules totally, for the cause of robustness, which makes the situation even worse. Furthermore, there are too many components in SMTP system, between which two would cause security problems. 4.2 Comprehensive Solutions to Avoid Spoofing Reject the mails which have ambiguous address problems. But in the real life, many mails are not perfectly right. So we can’t do it in the receiving server and receiving MUA. ⇒ We need to begin it in the sending MUA. Deploy IMAP extension to pass the authentication result to the MUA, instead of passing the result in the “Authentication-Results” header. This solution can also shield the Authentication-Result header to be spoofed. Add the verify result of identity. To solve “multi-identifier” problems, apart from adding “xxx: pass” to the Authentication-Result, we can also add”xxx_identifier: xxx” to the result. In this way, we can unify the identifiers used in different components. And thus we can decrease the inconsistency in a large scale ⇒ Authentication-Result header is safe enough ⇒ We can use Comprehensive_solutions_2 to help get it solved. Collect suspicious information. For mails that we have found to be abnormal, though we can accept it by courtesy of robustness, it is important to collect the information so that the MUA can display it to the user. Set detailed configuration of the MUA. To improve the complicated syntax rules which make much contribution to the difficulty of the interacting between different implementation, the service providers need to keep a configuration message, which signify the parts of the “FROM” rules, like white space、portion、notes. Improve the rules of “FROM” headers. We don’t chase restricting the input totally. Instead, we try to reduce the freedom of it. For example, we can request the header to be regular-language、context-free-language… to the lower level in the Chomsky infrastructure. We impose a same parser to both the receiving server and the MUA. In this way, we can extinguish the inconsistency in the parsing procedure. Besides, simply set some restrictions to part of the special characters, which lead to most bugs, like “(”. Realizing the inconsistency between the receiving server and the MUA. The service providers can eliminate the parsing & verifying procedure of the MUA. The receiving server pass the authentication result . This result will not be parsed/verified again. ⇒ RFC standards have considered this solution. But it is not defined well, which can still be spoofed. ⇒ choose some new transfer protocol like IMAP/POP. 4.3 Current Defenses Aims at the threat of spam spreading into the enterprise network and causing great harm. A large number of potential malicious spam are collected, and the main distinguishing features of dangerous spam are analyzed. The common supervised machine learning classifiers are used for learning test and optimization. Finally, the best performance model is obtained: Support Vector Machine & random forest classifiers. The recall rate is 91.6% and the accuracy rate is 95.2% Dynamic blacklist based on IP/domain/HELO/FROM. Blacklist is widely used in many places. It protect the user from too many malicious mails. In the practice, it is always combined with reporting. If your mails are reported to be dangerous by several people in a short time, all mails from you may be rejected, even there is spoofing. Over-sign technique. In a common case, there is only 1 header in the mail, so we will only check 1 header. But under the risk of spoofing with multi-header, we need to change the protocol. We make digital signature on all the possible headers, even on nonexistent headers. So any changes will fail. figure 4.1: An example of Over-sign technique working principle IMAP/POP extensions. In the past, the receiving server will parse and verify the mail, add the result to the mail and send it to the user. And the inconsistency between the receiving server and the MUA, is one of the major reasons of risks. After adopting the IMAP/POP extensions, your computers are really a screen for displaying. You don’t need to do the verifying for the second time. figure 4.2: An example of IMAP/POP extension working principle ARC protocol. Authentication Received chain. In this protocol, a mail is not allowed to be changed in the transit. Just like what the picture shows, if a Mail from ZJU get changed in the Yahoo, the user will search for the chain. If the chain is trustworthy, the user will accept the mail. figure 4.3: An example of ARC mail chain and trust chain 4.4 Other Possible Attacks and Solutions Homograph Impersonation: IDN (international domain name) allows people from all over the world to visit websites in their own national languages. In the field of Web defense, there has always been a way of attacking websites by means of profiling. Characters in many languages may look very similar. The classic one is the official website of apple. A can be replaced by characters in other languages, All the headers that may be presented in the address bar – from header, may be disguised as characters of other languages. This attack takes advantage of the homonym of human languages, so it is difficult to solve fundamentally Solutions: There are similarities and differences between e-mail system and web system. For example, both of them are domain based processing methods, and need to interact with DNS server. Therefore, we can learn from the browser’s solution and experience of IDN problem, and build similar filter verification components on MUA. However, we need to pay attention to that web system is often divided into mainstream websites. However, in the SMTP system, the mailbox name is no mainstream or substream, and the use frequency of individual users may be lower than that of attackers, so it can not be copied. It needs to be combined with the characteristics of the mail system. Appendix 1: Detail Solutions to Different Attacks A1+A2 HELO/MAIL_FROM confusion We should regard the SPF result to be “fail” if we can’t get the DNS-record for SPF policy. All DMARC implementation should be strict, in which way we can prevent bypassing through non-existent sub-domain. Use unified identifier in SPF and DMARC process. For example, we impose the SPF to verify “HELO” and pass”SPF_identifier: HELO” to the DMARC. And then DMARC can use the same identifier for verifying. Filter the characters in the MAIL_FROM header which may lead to ambiguity like “(” A3 ambiguous domains DMARC not only verify the “d=” label from the DKIM, but also verify the raw MAIL_FROM, and request these two to be unified. Upgrade the DNS server to avoid the “\x00”-cheating. This may lead to incremental deployment problems and cost a lot. A4+A5 SPF/DKIM injection We call for help from the DNS server just like what we do in A3.2 Recognize the special characters that the DNS server may not support before SPF/DKIM pass the content to the DNS server. And tell the exception to the Authentication-Result. Impose a same parser in SPF、DKIM and DMARC A3.1 A6 multiple FROM header Obey the RFC5322 standard and accept only one FROM header. A7 space-surrounded FORM header Improve the complicated syntax rules of FROM header, and in this way we can solve the problem while we don’t loose much robustness. A8 FROM alternative header When we look forward to other headers as substitutes of FROM header, we should notify the user instead of simply putting them in the address. Comprehensive_solutions.2: Passing the identifiers used in the receiving server. A9 Multiple email address Receiving server don’t insert the Sender to the FROM header, since the sender maybe not the real sender. The insertion may lead to the FROM header’s inconsistency between DMARC and MUA. A10 Email address encoding Since the problems arise from “Servers can’t distinguish non-ASCII-encoded From header”, we should improve the syntax library so the server can support them. Tell the MUA that the server don’t support other encoding methods, and impose the MUA to use the same encoding methods. ⇒ This way may lead to some decrease in the robustness A11 Route portion Sending server begin to unable route-portion function. And then the MUA should follow this action. Comprehensive solutions 5 A12 Quoted-pairs Comprehensive solutions 5 A13 Parsing inconsistency Comprehensive solutions 6.2 B1 Invisible characters Comprehensive solutions 4 Instead of using open-failure, we use open-reject. Failure of DNS request of SPF、DKIM、DMARC at the same time is impossible in the theory, and at that time ,we should reject the mail. The danger of the mail overwhelms the robustness principle. For domain datas that can’t be shown as characters like \u2000, we can display it in the address as byte-string, and then notify the user. B2 Encoding Comprehensive solutions 4 Instead of using open-failure, we use open-reject. Failure of DNS request of SPF、DKIM、DMARC at the same time is impossible in the theory, and at that time ,we should reject the mail. The danger of the mail overwhelms the robustness principle. For domain data that can’t be shown as characters like \u2000, we can display it in the address as byte-string, and then notify the user. B3 From alternative header When we find the FROM header to be null, we should just show it to be null, instead of showing another header as a substitute. And we need to notify the user about the possible senders. Just reject the mail, because FROM header is a very basic header which is so important that forgetting it seems to be impossible. A14+A15+A16 Header spoofing Get all the headers into the protection of DKIM signature. By Comprehensive solutions 7, we can solve the inconsistency problems between receiving server and the MUA. And based on this, we can still enjoy the robustness brought by the “I=” flag. combining (1) and (2) is reasonable and useful. A17 spoofing via an account Combine comprehensive solutions 1,4,5,6. We can mitigate the incremental deployment problem while decrease the complexity of FROM header. Mail service providers always deploy username/password authentication mechanism incompatible with the complex FROM header syntax. So there is no need to verify the previous one based on the latter one. We can extract the message from the username, if the From header is not so standard, we can replace the FROM header with the username, since the previous one can be more safe by courtesy of the service providers. This paper proposes to remove the authentication function of MUA directly and transfer the authentication responsibility to receiving server completely. MUA only exists as the front end and is not responsible for the actual authentication Therefore, the sender can not fill in the from header, but the sending receiver can fill in according to the user name / password, which can also avoid many security problems caused by the inconsistency between MUA and sending server A18 Replay attack to subvert DKIM signature Since MUA has verified the DKIM signature of the first from header, it can’t display the second from header, otherwise the verification will lose its function. The real address displayed by MUA should be parsed from the result of DKIM verification, not directly from the e-mail Adopt the solution of A17.3, let MUA not directly fill in the from header. In this way, attackers will not be able to directly manipulate the from header of e-mail, and naturally have no chance to exploit the inconsistency of multi from header vulnerability Using over-sign technology, we can avoid multi from header and have certain resistance to various attacks based on from header Appendix 2: Teamwork Team Member Work 刘佳润 Raise the topic. Reference researching. Demo implementation. Writing the report. 孙奥 Reference researching. Relative work analysis. Further study and research. Writing the report. 项俊程 Reference researching. Relative work analysis. Writing the report. 谢华洋 Reference researching. Writing the report. References Composition Kills: A Case Study of Email Sender Authentication - Jianjun Chen, Vern Paxson, Jian Jiang; 29th USENIX Security Symposium. Towards More Security in Data Exchange: Defining Unparsers with Context-Sensitive Encoders for Context-Free Grammars - Lars Hermerschmidt, Stephen Kugelmann, Bernhard Rumpe; 2015 IEEE Security and Privacy Workshops. IMAP/POP AUTHorize Extension for Simple Challenge/Response - J. Klensin, R. Catoe, P. Krumviede; RFC 2195, 1997. End-to-End Measurements of Email Spoofing Attacks - Hang Hu, Gang Wang; 27th USENIX Security Symposium. Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance (DMARC) - M. Kucherawy, Ed., E. Zwicky, Ed.; RFC 7489, 2015. Email Authentication Mechanisms: DMARC, SPF and DKIM - Stephen Nightingale.

title: NAD Reading Reports
text: Reading Report of NAD Original Paper: NEURAL ATTENTION DISTILLATION: ERASING BACK- DOOR TRIGGERS FROM DEEP NEURAL NETWORKS Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2021 Pre-Learning Teacher-student network: How to use a pretrained network (teacher network) to train another narrow but deeper network (student network). Also known as a technique in transfer learning. KD Algorithm (Knowledge Distillation): first proposed to compress a bigger or an ensemble of well-trained network(s) (teacher network) into a compact smaller network (student network). The algorithm helps the student network to learn more high-quality intermediate representations, leading to better performance. Attention Transfer: Attention Model is used to select the useful information rather than all. NAD: Neuron Attention Distillation The job of NAD is to align neurons that are more responsive to the trigger pattern with benign neurons that only responsible for meaningful representations. Attention Representation Three formulations of the attention operator all generates from the 3D activation map of the i-th channel. They represent the following info: All activation regions; Amplify the disparities between the backdoored neurons and the benign neurons by an order of p; Aligns the activation center of the backdoored neurons with that of the benign neurons by taking the mean over all activation regions. Attention Distillation Loss The teacher network is kept fixed throughout the distillation process. Overall Training Loss The combination of the CE loss and the sum of the NAD loss over all K residual groups: D is a subset of clean data used in finetuning. l is the index of the residual group, β is a hyperparameter controlling the strength. NAD Operation Principle Workflow The paper shows that NAD could use only 5% of clean training data and effectively erase the backdoor trigger. Two networks are initialized first. Initialize optimizer of momentum stochastic gradient descent. Define loss function with both cross entropy loss and attention transfer. Initialize training and testing data including poisoned data in testing data set. For each epoch, adjust the learning rate by supervising the learning turns. Three activation neurons and attention transfer loss are considered into the total loss. For each training step, activation function is operated to do the distillation. Understand the Distillation Neuron distillation is a method to implement the transfer learning. Take the attention map function of the reference code as an example. It’s an AT model with sum of absolute values with power p: 123456789101112131415class AT(nn.Module): def __init__(self, p): super(AT, self).__init__() self.p = p def forward(self, fm_s, fm_t): loss = F.mse_loss(self.attention_map(fm_s), self.attention_map(fm_t)) return loss def attention_map(self, fm, eps=1e-6): am = torch.pow(torch.abs(fm), self.p) am = torch.sum(am, dim=1, keepdim=True) norm = torch.norm(am, dim=(2,3), keepdim=True) am = torch.div(am, norm+eps) return am It’s exactly the attention maps using the Ap sum attention operator. Results and Evaluation The paper evaluate the performance of defense mechanisms with two metrics: attack success rate (ASR), which is the ratio of backdoored examples that are misclassified as the target label, and model’s accuracy on clean samples (ACC). The more the ASR drops and the less the ACC drops, the stronger the defense mechanism is. (TBC. More work and exploration will be done.)

title: 《全面战争：三国》分析评测
text: 《全面战争：三国》分析评价报告 基本信息 游戏名称：《Total War™: THREE KINGDOMS》 中文译名：《全面战争：三国》 开发商：CREATIVE ASSEMBLY 发行商：世嘉（SEGA） 本体发售时间：2019年5月23日（最新的DLC仍在开发与发售中，2021年3月11日即将上市） 游戏类型：RTS + TSG （回合战略 + 即时战略 = 大战略） 本体价格：￥268（不含DLC与扩展包） 最低硬件配置需求 Windows Mac OS Steam OS + Linux 操作系统 Win7 64位 macOS 10.14.4 Ubuntu 18.04 64-bit 处理器 Intel Core 2 Duo 3.00Ghz 2GHz Intel Core i5 3.4GHz Intel Core i3-4130 内存 4 GB RAM 8 GB RAM 6 GB RAM 显卡 GTX 650 Ti 1GB / HD 7850 1GB / Intel UHD Graphics 620 2GB Nvidia 680MX / 2GB AMD R9 M290 / 1.5GB Intel Iris Graphics 540 2GB AMD R9 285 (GCN 3rd Generation) / 2GB Nvidia GTX 680 DirectX版本 11 - - 存储空间 至少60GB 至少52GB 至少60GB 推荐硬件配置： Windows Mac OS Steam OS + Linux 操作系统 Win10 64位 macOS 10.14.4 Ubuntu 18.04 64-bit 处理器 Intel i5-6600 / Ryzen 5 2600X 2GHz Intel Core i5 3.4GHz Intel Core i3-4130 内存 8 GB RAM 8 GB RAM 8 GB RAM 显卡 GTX 960 / R9 Fury X 4GB VRAM 2GB Nvidia 680MX / 2GB AMD R9 M290 / 1.5GB Intel Iris Graphics 540 或更好 4GB AMD RX 480 / 4GB Nvidia GTX 970 DirectX版本 11 - - 存储空间 至少60GB 至少60GB 至少60GB 本人测评使用的硬件配置 操作系统：Windows 10 64位 CPU：Intel i5-7300Q 内存：8 GB RAM 显卡：GTX 1050 DirectX版本：11 游戏概要 简要概述 ​ 从Steam商店平台的介绍中，可以看到这是一款延续了“全面战争”系列游戏风格的，主打中国历史题材“三国”的大型策略游戏。之前放眼于“罗马战争”、“中世纪战争”、“匈奴王阿提拉”等恢弘背景与主题的“全战”系列，这次选择中国历史上最为动荡纷争、最为百花缭乱的三国时期作为主题，确实是全面战争系列的一个历史性的里程碑。 故事情节 游戏本体剧情 ​ 游戏本体从公元190年的中国开始。经历了汉末朝堂内外的纷争，西凉军阀董卓掌握了朝廷，凭借其帐下文武势力，烧杀抢掠，废立皇帝，无恶不作。以曹操、袁绍为首的各路诸侯组成联军起兵讨伐，董卓火烧长安，西奔洛阳。联军初见成就，但是各路诸侯各怀异心，联军名存实亡。 ​ 你将扮演这些英雄豪杰中的一员，在乱世中选择自己的王道，完成自己的夙愿。 ​ 可选择派系：“三国”：曹操、刘备、孙坚；“军阀（诸侯）”：袁绍、董卓、袁术、公孙瓒；“汉臣”：刘表、孔融、马腾、陶谦；“盗贼”：张燕、郑姜、严白虎；“黄巾余党”（DLC人物）：龚都、黄邵、何仪。 DLC《Eight Princess（八王之乱）》剧情 ​ 故事发生在重归一统之后的西晋，与三国关系不大，虽然派系和玩法有很大的更新，但是评价不高，不做介绍。 DLC《Mandate of Heaven（受命于天）》剧情 ​ 描述了公元182年开始的汉帝国内部的派系纷乱、诸侯军阀势力的崛起、黄巾义军的热情燃遍中原的年代。 ​ 这个DLC补全了游戏本体剧情的前因，构建了完整的“三国历史”。你将体验大汉帝国的内忧外患，黄巾义军的豪情壮志，保皇党的忠贞不屈，以及各路诸侯的传奇开端。 ​ 可选择新派系：“朝廷”：汉灵帝；“保皇派”：刘宠、卢植；“黄巾义军”：张角、张宝、张梁。 DLC《A World Betrayed（弃叛之世）》剧情 ​ 故事发生在公元190年。在这之前，吕布诛杀义父董卓，孙策之父孙坚遇刺身亡，曹操之父曹嵩死于陶谦手下。年少的孩子总是活在父辈的阴影下，然而真正决定命运的是他们长大成人的选择。是走出弑父的恶名成就全新的霸业，还是为父报仇延续父亲的威名，还是把握时机，缔造自己的传奇和未来，历史的拐点来到了玩家的手中。 ​ 可选择新派系：吕布、孙策。 DLC《The Furious Wild（狂野南蛮）》剧情 ​ 故事发生在远离中原的蛮荒之地——南中。地图扩展到了古代中国的最南端。在与中原文化完全不一样的这里，玩家将如何一统南境诸部族，建立强大的南蛮王国？ ​ 这个DLC是一次非常庞大的扩充，对于地图、派系、战斗部队、升级机制等都有了全新的更新，针对南蛮文化也设立了全新的任务和事件，并加入了许多全新的派系。 ​ 可选择新派系：“南蛮”：孟获、祝融、沙摩柯、木鹿；“汉臣”：士燮。 DLC《Fates Devided（暂译：命运分别）》剧情 ​ 故事发生在公元200年。吕布已诛，逐鹿中原的霸主只剩下了曹操与袁绍。这一对昔日的战友，如今已经是剑拔弩张的对手。你可以选择扮演曹操，重演官渡之战后一扫中原的曹魏霸业，也可以选择扮演袁绍，重写河北枭雄染指江山的全新历史。当然也可以选择其他英雄人物，体验三国历史这一段群雄争鸣的历史。 ​ 可选择新派系：“汉臣”：刘焉。 玩家角色与任务 玩家可扮演角色已经在上面提到 ​ 游戏的任务系统与以往的“全面战争”系列有很大的不同：一般派系只要达成：派系声望（爵位）升级到王；击败另外两家称帝的势力并夺取其首都；即可宣告战役胜利。 ​ 但是随着游戏的DLC的发型，系统的扩展、剧情和派系的完善与进步，任务呈现出了多样化的效果。例如在《受命于天》中，扮演汉灵帝巩固了皇位，并在与黄巾军的战争中取得一定比例的上风就可以宣告镇压成功并维护皇权；扮演张氏兄弟只要在战争中取得一定比例的上风并与黄巾军共同控制相当大的领土就可以宣告起义胜利。在《弃叛之世》中，扮演吕布在战斗中打倒指定的英雄人物，就可以宣告胜利。在《狂野南蛮》中，扮演南蛮派系统一所有南蛮部族即可宣告胜利。 游戏系统 地图 ​ 游戏复现了东汉三国时期的中国地图，在几次更新与改版后，甚至可以精确到郡县。地形方面也尽可能真实地还原出来。游戏中可以通过鼠标滚轮的拉动缩放，实现2D与3D的变化。 ​ 人物与军队以战棋的形式在地图中行动，并且会有相应的人物动画。 内政与建设 ​ 游戏中的资源类型主要是金钱和军粮。而金钱的来源除了抢掠战斗，最大的来源就是税收。对根据地（城池）内的建筑进行建设与升级，可以提升声望、也可以获得相应的产出效果。比如农田会在每回合生产军粮，而市场会在每回合生产金钱。同时一些军队类建筑、教育类建筑、政治类建筑都会有相应的加成。 ​ 腐败有时会成为经济停滞的一大难点。这一点过于真实——所以指派将领去进行纠察、建立相应的建筑来减少腐败也是相当重要的。民心也是保证稳定的一大属性，想办法在城镇里树立民心，稳定百姓的情绪也是十分重要的。 科技树与改革 ​ 不同流派（汉朝的诸侯、南蛮、黄巾军、匪徒）的科技树不同。点亮科技树，会解锁新的建筑、军队，获得新的加成等等。 外交系统 ​ 全战三国一改其他三国游戏中外交系统的鸡肋，成为了游戏中相当重要的一大系统。签订互不侵犯协议、组织联军、构建同盟，将会使得你的安全地位稳定；开展贸易、交换资源、进行买卖，将会使你与诸侯之间的关系以及自己的经济来源得到保障；宣战、建立帝国、设立附庸，则是你开启宏图霸业的开始。 ​ 有时，有钱能使鬼推磨，足够丰厚的财政，也会使其他诸侯对你的宣战化险为夷。 战斗系统 ​ 全面战争的“大战略”即体现在回合制的“战役”，又体现在即时性的“战斗”。指挥千军万马的排兵布阵，设定攻守布局，利用将领之间的默契、羁绊，以及将领的个人特性与技能，实现战斗的胜利，是玩家在战斗中的目标。 ​ 全战三国的战斗系统相当丰富，同时也提高了玩家对于多各方面的考虑的要求，包括士兵的数量、将领的生命值、防守建筑的位置、部队的士气等等。有时尽管将领冲锋陷阵杀敌无数，但是后方部队遭到了敌军的包抄，也可能导致全军士气降低到0而失败。 ​ 战斗中对于操作性有一定的要求，主要体现在宏观方面，对于“微操”要求并不是很高，不需要很精湛的键鼠配合技术。 事件 ​ 游戏中跟随时间推进出现的固定事件以及可能会出现的随机事件有时会提供一些不同的选项，这些将决定了玩家的游戏走向，很可能开启一段全新的历史。游戏的难度、派系之间关系的发展，都可能发生翻天覆地的变化，也使得游戏的可玩性大大加强了。 艺术性 ​ 游戏的画风非常赞。 ​ 水墨风格的背景图，与山水国画相仿的地图设计，以及充满了中国古代特色的UI设计，使得游戏的艺术性收到了一致的好评。 ​ 人物形象的原画也非常有特色，在写实的基础上融入了制作者对于人物形象的理解和油画风格，人设比较丰满与贴切。 音乐 ​ 从宣传片全中文的演唱，就可以体现制作方的诚意以及音乐的优质。 ​ 游戏的BGM在用交响乐展现恢弘磅礴的气势的同时，也融入了许多中国传统乐器，比如二胡、琵琶、古筝、竹笛、传统打击乐等，用以烘托气氛。富有浓郁的历史感、民族感。以片头BGM为例，在锣声引入后，小提琴的音响与背后民族弹拨乐的交织，拉开了这段风云莫测的历史帷幕，随后便是浑厚的铜管奏响主题曲，“江山如画，一时多少豪杰”的氛围顿时腾然升起。竹笛空灵的旋律与号角激昂的鸣响相交替，最后由胡琴的收尾，仿佛在讲述“合久必分分久必合”的历史宿命，让人又有怅然若失而回味无穷的感觉。 ​ 游戏中的原声音乐选取大多与游戏进展和时间发生相吻合，让玩家的沉浸式体验非常美好。有时会注意不到背景音乐的优美，而在停下来欣赏的时候，又会让人有如痴如醉的感觉。 游戏评价 注：在优缺点评价中，很大一部分加入了个人的见解和体验，不一定保证客观 技术的体现 游戏AI（其他派系） ​ 《全战三国》的一大亮点体现在派系的独特性。例如刘备这种“仁德之君”，在各诸侯之间都颇有好感，并且可以凭借自己的“齐心”之力，兵不血刃地接管汉帝国的领土；而曹操这种“乱世奸雄”，则可以凭借自己的“信誉”，操控诸侯之间的关系，乃至于挑起离间战争；而诸如严白虎之徒，则是见钱眼开的匪徒，乐意为出价高的军阀当雇佣兵，借此获得财富与声誉。 ​ 但是这些派系特点并不一定体现在玩家手里。其他派系作为AI出现时，也相当忠实地还原了其派系特点。回合制游戏里能够体现其他对手的内心行动，而不至于将其退化成为“愚蠢的电脑”，即使是单人游戏也仿佛在于其他与你旗鼓相当甚至略胜一筹的对手进行博弈。 战斗引擎 ​ 全面战争系列的万人战也使得它无愧于“大战略”的标签。 ​ 地形是战斗中最重要的部分之一。RTS游戏的地形需要和第一人称游戏有所不同，更着重于中长距离，而不是短距离。全战的战斗引擎打破了地表模式，在不同地形类型之间有平滑过渡，也支持即时传送（当玩家点击迷你地图某处时），这一点得益于流媒体技术的应用。另外在地图编辑中也有大量的自定义网格（例如悬崖碎片），这需要与地形纹理相融合。 ​ 另外全面战争的渲染使用了3个GBuffer纹理，在前两个纹理的alpha通道里不储存任何内容，而是在渲染地形和贴图的时候将所有的属性融合在一起，用alpha通道来指定alpha数量，法线则以双通道压缩的格式存储。光照渲染则是主要来自定向光和基于BRDF模型的统计。整体效果来看已经相当惊艳。 亮点与优点 全面战争+三国=东西方艺术的碰撞。相比较之前由日本光荣公司的系列三国作品（《三国志》系列、《三国无双》系列），这一款更加有3A大作的风格，也更有西式游戏的特点，同时其文化背景、艺术表现都充分体现了中华文化和历史的特点，这种交融并没有让人感到强烈的违和感，相反显得相得益彰，乃至于别有风味。 作为一款以三国历史为背景的游戏，比较尊重历史与小说，做了相当多的考据，对于三国这个IP的受众和爱好者而言，是不可多得的佳作。 派系的特征非常新颖，也比较符合历史形象，是非常有趣的游戏体验！ 全面战争系列强调的“大战略”的复杂性与综合性使得这款游戏大概是所有三国主题游戏中最为丰富和复杂的一款。但是对于初接触全面战争系列的玩家（比如我）来说，游戏合理设置了难度的层级（比如战役难度和战斗难度分离，各个派系的初始难度不一样），使得玩家易于上手，由浅入深，层层递进，欲罢不能。 设立了“史实模式”和“演义模式”，面向多种游戏玩家群体。在“演义模式”中，你的武将技能有大幅的提升，在战斗当中甚至可以以一敌百，一夫当关，万夫莫开，英雄将领的气质与魅力得到极大的彰显。然而在“史实模式”中，将领与士兵近乎无异，只有少数属性和技能上的提升，这更考验玩家排兵布阵、兵种相克、战术与策略的布置和选择。 缺点 强行的游戏性使得真实性欠缺，以至于出现许多“邪魔”玩法。 复杂的人物建模使得不能够将所有人物得以呈现，大量“大众脸”人物以及姓名的出现令人感到不适。再加之AI人名的设计和很差的翻译令人体验很糟糕，或者说感到滑稽。 人物建模还是采用全战的老引擎，在建模上人物不太符合东方审美，有些过于写实以至于不太好看。 与三国历史的贴合度不够高。虽然在游戏初期尽可能的还原了派系关系、人物关系与特性，但是在游戏的自由度影响下，会出现很多让人大跌眼镜的局势演变。而过少的与历史相关的剧情事件，使得后期不像是在玩一款三国游戏。 与其他游戏的对比 ​ 要评价《全面战争：三国》，就不得不和当时与其几乎是同时发行的另一款大IP三国游戏对比——日本光荣公司的《三国志14》（2020年年初发行）。 画面：全战三国完全胜利——全战系列的招牌战斗引擎所渲染的宏大的万人站是三国志的模拟所完全不能匹及的。 画风：各有千秋。同时作为《三国志》系列的老玩家，我对于光荣《三国志》系列的人物设定和绘制风格也是非常喜爱，但这并不能说哪一方更胜一筹，因为人物设定是由所差异的。从个人爱好而言，三国志的画风我更喜欢一点。 配音：三国志系列那种继承日本动漫风格的配音让人有些难以接受，违和感很强。全战三国专门请了专业的配音演员进行配音，汉语配音大好评！ 游戏性：从《三国志11》以后，三国志的战略玩法越来越想学习全面战争，结果就是《三国志14》饱受诟病。全战三国的战略和战斗分离、大战役兼顾多局，这一点比三国志系列要做的好得多。 历史背景：这一点上，我认为还是《三国志》系列更好。毕竟全战三国是“披着三国皮的全战”，即便加入了许多的三国元素，也考虑了人物特性、派系特点等，但是总体而言还是与三国历史的切合度略差一点。而《三国志》在战役中插入了更多事件，与历史或小说相贴合，更加还原历史。再加上光荣开发团队深耕多年的三国历史，包括人物介绍、关系、特性、事件处理以及对三国的理解仍然是当今游戏厂商中最优秀的。 价格：都好贵，但是《三国志14》更贵。那么肯定是买全战三国玩啦！ 个人评价 惊艳的全战，特别的三国。 ​ 总的而言，这款作品对得上它“特别好评”的用户评价，也对得起它的价格。 烧机的3A大作 ​ 从下图这中可以看出，在最高分辨率下，至少要RTX20系列的显卡才可以支持比较流畅的视觉体验。而固态硬盘又几乎是这款游戏的刚需，否则很难有最佳的战斗体验。因此，配置不好的机子很可能是跑不动这款游戏的。 诚意满满的佳作 ​ 为了制作出合格的游戏，CA专门在设计方面下了苦功夫。比如游戏中士兵、人物的铠甲绘制，都是经过了认真的考据的，所以与历史原型较为吻合。这款游戏虽然体现的是西方人眼里的三国，但是，这也不是无根无据的想象，而是诚意满满的再现。 略显遗憾的三国游戏 ​ 从三国题材的角度考虑，这款游戏在发行初期显然还是存在诸多不足。不过经历几次更新、DLC与地图扩充、地图细化、章节更新、派系细化，已经有所改善，但是仍然是全战内核的三国游戏。对于文化和历史性的挖掘和保持还是略逊一筹。但是瑕不掩瑜，如此丰富乃至于“过于真实”的游戏性，已经足够让人称赞了。
